title: Raw user prompt export via OpenTelemetry
why_it_matters: Codex's OpenTelemetry (OTel) log export can track Codex runs, including API requests, SSE/events, prompts, and tool approvals/results, but OpenAI's documentation states OTel export is disabled by default and must be opted into via the [otel] table, whose documented example configures exporter = "none" (send nothing). Within that table, otel.log_user_prompt controls whether raw user prompts are included in any exported OTel logs; the documented example sets log_user_prompt = false with the comment "redact user prompts unless explicitly enabled." This rule observes only the otel.log_user_prompt flag: it does not inspect otel.exporter or otel.trace_exporter, so an enabled flag has no effect while every OTel exporter remains at its documented "none" default, and other configuration layers are not inspected.

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config keeps raw user prompts redacted from OpenTelemetry logs (otel.log_user_prompt = false).

outcomes.when: otel.log_user_prompt is explicitly true in the user-level config
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config explicitly opts in to exporting raw user prompts with OpenTelemetry logs (otel.log_user_prompt = true).

remediation.summary: Stop exporting raw user prompts via OpenTelemetry.

outcomes.when: otel.log_user_prompt is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective OpenTelemetry raw prompt export posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: otel.log_user_prompt is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented example redacts raw prompts from OpenTelemetry logs unless explicitly enabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.

outcomes.when: otel.log_user_prompt is set to a value outside boolean true/false
outcomes.message: otel.log_user_prompt is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: otel.log_user_prompt is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Codex configuration reference
sources.notes: Documents otel.log_user_prompt as a boolean key: "Opt in to exporting raw user prompts with OpenTelemetry logs." Re-verified 2026-07-17; page content unchanged since the 2026-07-16 retrieval cited by codex-history-persist-01 (identical normalized content hash).

sources.title: Codex advanced configuration
sources.notes: States, under "Observability and telemetry": "Enable OpenTelemetry (OTel) log export to track Codex runs (API requests, SSE/events, prompts, tool approvals/results). Disabled by default; opt in via [otel]:", followed by an example configuring exporter = "none" and log_user_prompt = false with the comment "redact user prompts unless explicitly enabled." Re-verified 2026-07-17; page content unchanged since the 2026-07-16 retrieval cited by codex-history-persist-01 (identical normalized content hash).

limitations: Only the user-level CODEX_HOME/config.toml is inspected; system config, selected profiles, trusted-project config, and CLI overrides are not inspected.
An explicit user-level value describes that layer only and does not prove the effective value for every profile, trusted project, or invocation.

This rule does not inspect otel.exporter or otel.trace_exporter; OpenAI's documentation states OTel export is disabled by default, so an enabled otel.log_user_prompt has no effect unless an exporter is also configured.
This rule cannot confirm any server-side or remote retention of exported OTel data — no local file can.
Auth method (ChatGPT sign-in vs API key) changes data-policy interpretation and is user-confirmed-or-unknown; this rule never infers it.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
otel.log_user_prompt is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
otel.log_user_prompt set to a value outside boolean true/false (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
